context.description: Reflective prose on perception and awareness, arguing that bodily impulses and forgetting shape action. It introduces the section "Üben mach frei" and gives an exercise for separating eye movement from conscious attention in a busy public space.
normalization_notes: Born-digital book page with continuous prose and an attention exercise.

rows.paragraph_text: Reflective passage on how bodily impulses, perception, and forgetting influence behavior.
Instructional exercise: fix the eyes on a distant point while letting attention move across the surroundings to feel the difference between sight and awareness.

content.free_text: book_page consciousness_and_mind philosophy_of_consciousness Book page from Bewusstseinsreisen - Version 2 on consciousness, perception, and attention. Section: Practice makes free. Includes a reflective argument about bodily impulses and an exercise for separating eye movement from conscious attention.
